preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

## Coordination
- Read COORDINATION.md before starting any work
- Update COORDINATION.md after completing your work with:
  - What you did
  - Key decisions and their rationale
  - Warnings or context for the next agent
  - Status of your assigned tasks
preSessionState.COORDINATION.md:
# Coordination Log

Use this file to share decisions, status updates, and context with other agents.
Write your updates below. There is no enforced format — use whatever structure helps.

---

## Agent 1 — Notification Architecture Unification (Complete)

**Decision:** Unified all notification triggers to use **EventBus**, removing the `CallbackRegistry` pattern for notifications.

**What changed:**
- `src/services/order.service.ts` — `updateOrderStatus` now uses `eventBus.emit()` instead of `statusChangeCallbacks.notify()`. Removed `callback-registry` import.
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — `registerHandlers()` now subscribes to both `order:created` and `order:status-changed` via `eventBus.on()`. Removed `callback-registry` import.
- `src/utils/callback-registry.ts` — Removed the `statusChangeCallbacks` singleton. The generic `CallbackRegistry` class is retained for other potential uses.
- `tests/services/notification.service.test.ts` — Rewrote all status-change tests to use `eventBus.emit()` instead of `statusChangeCallbacks.notify()`. Added test for unified architecture (both event types through EventBus).
- `tests/services/order.service.test.ts` — Updated assertion to verify status-changed events now appear in EventBus log (2 events instead of 1).
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — New file documenting the decision, rationale, and how to extend.

**Key context for other agents:**
- The `statusChangeCallbacks` export no longer exists. Any code importing it will break.
- All notification-related events must go through EventBus — this is now the single pattern.
- TypeScript compiles cleanly; all 72 tests pass.

postSessionState.COORDINATION.md:
# Coordination Log

Use this file to share decisions, status updates, and context with other agents.
Write your updates below. There is no enforced format — use whatever structure helps.

---

## Agent 1 — Notification Architecture Unification (Complete)

**Decision:** Unified all notification triggers to use **EventBus**, removing the `CallbackRegistry` pattern for notifications.

**What changed:**
- `src/services/order.service.ts` — `updateOrderStatus` now uses `eventBus.emit()` instead of `statusChangeCallbacks.notify()`. Removed `callback-registry` import.
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — `registerHandlers()` now subscribes to both `order:created` and `order:status-changed` via `eventBus.on()`. Removed `callback-registry` import.
- `src/utils/callback-registry.ts` — Removed the `statusChangeCallbacks` singleton. The generic `CallbackRegistry` class is retained for other potential uses.
- `tests/services/notification.service.test.ts` — Rewrote all status-change tests to use `eventBus.emit()` instead of `statusChangeCallbacks.notify()`. Added test for unified architecture (both event types through EventBus).
- `tests/services/order.service.test.ts` — Updated assertion to verify status-changed events now appear in EventBus log (2 events instead of 1).
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — New file documenting the decision, rationale, and how to extend.

**Key context for other agents:**
- The `statusChangeCallbacks` export no longer exists. Any code importing it will break.
- All notification-related events must go through EventBus — this is now the single pattern.
- TypeScript compiles cleanly; all 72 tests pass.

## Agent 2 — Email Notification Handler (Complete)

**What changed:**
- `src/notifications/email-handler.ts` — New `EmailNotificationHandler` class that subscribes to `order:created` and `order:status-changed` events via EventBus. Formats both HTML and plain-text email bodies with status-specific messaging. Uses an `EmailTransport` interface for sending (with `InMemoryEmailTransport` for testing/demo).
- `tests/notifications/email-handler.test.ts` — 13 tests covering order confirmation emails, status-change emails for each status (confirmed, shipped, delivered, cancelled), multi-item support, sequential status changes, and transport functionality.

**Key decisions:**
- Used the `EmailTransport` interface pattern so the handler is decoupled from the actual email delivery mechanism. `InMemoryEmailTransport` is provided for testing; a real SMTP/API transport can be swapped in.
- Email body includes both `htmlBody` and `textBody` fields for proper email formatting.
- Status-specific messages (e.g., "Your order has been shipped and is on its way") provide better UX than generic status change text.
- Follows the same EventBus subscription pattern as `NotificationService` — no new patterns introduced.

**Key context for other agents:**
- The `EmailNotificationHandler` is a standalone handler that listens on the same EventBus events as `NotificationService`. Both can coexist — `NotificationService` handles in-app notifications, `EmailNotificationHandler` handles email.
- TypeScript compiles cleanly; all 85 tests pass (72 existing + 13 new).
